title: Tactile Medical Strengthens Board of Directors with Appointment of Andrea Pearson
author: Not Specified
description: Tactile Systems Technology, Inc. (Nasdaq: TCMD) announced the appointment of Andrea Pearson to its Board of Directors effective March 9, 2026. Pearson is an experienced healthcare executive with over 25 years in the industry, most recently serving as Growth Advisor and Chief Marketing Officer at Harbor Health. She previously held senior roles at DispatchHealth and HealthGrades, where she demonstrated strong track records in scaling businesses and driving revenue growth.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The appointment of an accomplished executive with proven expertise in healthcare scaling and growth strategy strengthens the company's leadership and signals confidence in future expansion. The board addition is expected to support the company's market leadership in lymphatic dysfunction and airway clearance therapies.

title: Stock Market Today: S&P 500, Dow, Nasdaq Futures Jump Ahead Of January FOMC Minutes—Palo Alto Networks, Tactile Systems, DoorDash In Focus (UPDATED)
author: Rishabh Mishra
description: U.S. stock futures rose on Wednesday with the Dow Jones, S&P 500, and Nasdaq 100 all posting gains. Investors await January FOMC minutes and Friday's PCE index release. Key movers include Ovintiv surging 4.17% after announcing a $3 billion asset sale, Tactile Systems jumping 23.53% on strong guidance, Celanese rising 5.94% despite earnings miss, and Palo Alto Networks falling 7.34% after lowering profit guidance.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Stock rose 4.17% following announcement of a definitive agreement to sell Anadarko assets for $3 billion in cash, demonstrating positive market reaction to the asset sale deal.
Stock surged 23.53% after reporting Q4 earnings and forecasting 8-11% revenue growth for 2026, despite potential Medicare headwinds, indicating strong market confidence in the company's outlook.
Stock soared 5.94% despite missing Q4 earnings expectations, driven by positive forward guidance with Q1 adjusted earnings forecast of 70-85 cents per share.
Stock tumbled 7.34% after lowering fiscal 2026 full-year profit guidance to $3.65-$3.70 per share, overshadowing a quarterly earnings beat and revenue outlook increase.
Stock was up only 0.30% ahead of earnings announcement with expected EPS of 59 cents and revenue of $3.99 billion, showing minimal market movement despite upcoming results.
ETF tracking the S&P 500 was up 0.57% in premarket trading, reflecting broader market gains across major indices.
ETF tracking the Nasdaq 100 advanced 0.62% in premarket trading, showing strength in technology and growth-oriented stocks.

title: Tactile Systems Stock Smashes 52-Week High After Strong Quarter
author: Vandana Singh
description: Tactile Systems Technology (NASDAQ: TCMD) surged 23.35% in premarket trading to a new 52-week high following strong fourth-quarter earnings and the acquisition of LymphaTech. The company reported Q4 EPS of 46 cents (beating consensus of 44 cents) and sales of $103.59 million (up 21%, exceeding estimates of $93.80 million). The $6.8 million acquisition is expected to enhance lymphedema treatment capabilities. For fiscal 2026, the company forecasts sales of $357-$365 million and adjusted EBITDA of $49-$51 million, both above consensus expectations.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Strong fourth-quarter earnings beat (46 cents vs. 44 cents consensus), significant revenue growth of 21% exceeding estimates, improved profitability metrics (gross profit +26%, operating income +50%), strategic acquisition to expand capabilities, and raised 2026 guidance above consensus expectations. Stock trading at 52-week high with 23.35% premarket gain and Buy rating from analysts.

title: Tactile Medical Acquires LymphaTech, Expanding Breadth and Depth of its Lymphedema Solutions Portfolio
author: Globe Newswire
description: Tactile Systems Technology Inc. (NASDAQ: TCMD) has acquired LymphaTech Inc. for $6.8 million upfront plus potential milestone-based payments. LymphaTech's 3D digital measurement platform for lymphedema assessment will expand Tactile Medical's lymphedema portfolio, enabling more accurate disease identification and progression monitoring. The acquisition positions Tactile Medical as a comprehensive solutions leader for lymphatic dysfunction.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The acquisition strengthens Tactile Medical's market position in lymphedema treatment, expands its product portfolio with advanced 3D measurement technology, enhances R&D capabilities, and positions the company as a comprehensive solutions leader. The deal enables the company to serve 20 million symptomatic patients in the U.S. and supports its evolution from a product-based to an integrated solutions company.

title: 3 Small Caps Drawing Insider and Institutional Support
author: Chris Markoch
description: The article highlights three small-cap stocks with potential for growth in 2025, focusing on insider and institutional buying amid improving macroeconomic conditions and potential interest rate cuts.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Strong 5% year-over-year same-store sales growth, projecting 13% total net sales growth, and stock up 35% in last 12 months
Steady revenue growth but not consistently profitable, stock down 41% in 2025, with potential in large underpenetrated medical device market
